description: Add full table support to Sidedoc - extract, preserve, and sync Word tables using GFM pipe table syntax

userStories.title: Create table test fixtures
userStories.description: As a developer, I need test fixtures with tables so that automated tests can verify the feature.
acceptanceCriteria: Create tests/fixtures/tables_simple.docx with basic 3x3 table (header + 2 data rows)
Table has columns: Name, Role, Start Date with sample data
Document fixture contents in test file comments
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Fixture created at tests/fixtures/tables_simple.docx. Test: test_tables_simple_fixture_exists.

userStories.title: Extract simple tables to GFM markdown
userStories.description: As an AI developer, I want tables extracted as GFM pipe tables so that I can read tabular data efficiently.
acceptanceCriteria: Tables in docx are extracted as GFM pipe table syntax in content.md
Access tables via document.tables and iterate rows/cells
Each table becomes a block with type 'table' in structure.json
Empty cells are preserved as empty pipe segments '| |'
Unit tests verify extraction with tables_simple.docx fixture
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented table_to_gfm() and refactored extract_blocks() to iterate over document body in order. Tests: test_extract_simple_table_to_gfm, test_extract_table_creates_table_block_type, test_extract_table_preserves_empty_cells.

userStories.title: Store table structure metadata in structure.json
userStories.description: As a developer, I need table structure stored so that tables can be reconstructed accurately.
acceptanceCriteria: structure.json stores rows, cols for each table block
structure.json stores cells array with row, col, content_hash per cell
docx_table_index field maps to original table position
Unit tests verify structure schema compliance
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added table_metadata to Block model. Implemented extract_table_metadata(). Tests: test_table_block_has_rows_cols_metadata, test_table_block_has_cells_metadata, test_table_block_has_docx_table_index.

userStories.title: Store basic table formatting in styles.json
userStories.description: As a developer, I need table formatting stored so that visual appearance survives roundtrip.
acceptanceCriteria: styles.json stores table_style (Word built-in style name if present)
styles.json stores column_widths array in inches
styles.json stores table_alignment (left, center, right)
Unit tests verify formatting extraction
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added table_formatting to Style model. Implemented extract_table_formatting(). Refactored extract_styles() to iterate document body in order. Tests: test_table_style_has_column_widths, test_table_style_has_table_alignment.

userStories.title: Reconstruct basic tables in docx
userStories.description: As a user, I want tables in the rebuilt docx to match the original structure.
acceptanceCriteria: GFM pipe table syntax converts to docx tables via doc.add_table()
Column widths match original from styles.json
Cell text content matches markdown
Unit tests verify tables appear in rebuilt docx
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented parse_gfm_table(), create_table_from_gfm(), updated parse_markdown_to_blocks() to detect tables. Tests: test_parse_markdown_detects_table_blocks, test_create_docx_creates_table, test_reconstruct_table_preserves_content.

userStories.title: Extract column alignment from tables
userStories.description: As a developer, I need column alignment preserved in the GFM syntax.
acceptanceCriteria: Cell horizontal alignment extracted from docx cells
Alignment represented using GFM syntax (:---|, :---:|, ---:|)
Default alignment (left) used when not specified
Unit tests verify alignment extraction and representation
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented get_cell_alignment(), get_column_alignments(), alignment_to_gfm_separator(). Added column_alignments to table_metadata. Tests: test_table_gfm_has_separator_row, test_table_metadata_has_column_alignments.

userStories.title: Apply column alignment during reconstruction
userStories.description: As a user, I want column alignment preserved in rebuilt tables.
acceptanceCriteria: Parse GFM alignment indicators from header separator row
Apply horizontal alignment to all cells in each column
Alignment survives roundtrip (extract -> build)
Unit tests verify alignment application
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented parse_gfm_alignments(). Updated parse_gfm_table() to return alignments. Updated create_table_from_gfm() to apply alignment. Tests: test_parse_gfm_alignment_indicators, test_reconstruct_table_applies_alignment.

userStories.title: Handle special characters in table cells
userStories.description: As a developer, I need special characters escaped so markdown parsing works correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Pipe characters in cell content escaped as \|
Backticks in cell content preserved correctly

Newlines in cells converted to
or handled gracefully
Unit tests cover special character escaping
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented escape_cell_content_for_gfm(). Pipes escaped as \|, newlines converted to spaces. Tests: test_extract_table_escapes_pipe_characters, test_extract_table_handles_newlines_in_cells.

userStories.title: Create formatted table test fixture
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a fixture with formatted tables for testing borders and shading.
acceptanceCriteria: Create tests/fixtures/tables_formatted.docx with styled table
Table has colored header row with borders
Table has alternating row shading
Document fixture contents in test file comments
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Fixture created at tests/fixtures/tables_formatted.docx with blue header row (D9E2F3), accent borders (4472C4), and alternating gray shading (F2F2F2). Test: test_tables_formatted_fixture_exists.

userStories.title: Extract cell background colors
userStories.description: As a user, I want cell colors preserved so that formatted tables look correct.
acceptanceCriteria: Cell background/shading color extracted as hex value
Colors stored in cell_styles in styles.json keyed by 'row,col'
Only non-default cells are stored (compact representation)
Unit tests verify color extraction
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented extract_cell_shading() in extract.py. Cell styles stored in table_formatting.cell_styles keyed by 'row,col'. Tests: test_extract_cell_background_colors, test_extract_cell_shading_only_stores_non_default.

userStories.title: Apply cell background colors during reconstruction
userStories.description: As a user, I want cell colors applied in rebuilt tables.
acceptanceCriteria: Read cell_styles from styles.json during rebuild
Apply background_color to cells using cell shading API
Colors survive roundtrip (extract -> build)
Unit tests verify color application
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented apply_cell_shading() and _apply_cell_styles() in reconstruct.py. Validates hex color format. Tests: test_reconstruct_applies_cell_background_colors, test_formatted_table_roundtrip.

userStories.title: Extract cell border styles
userStories.description: As a user, I want all border styles preserved so that tables look identical.
acceptanceCriteria: All four borders (top, bottom, left, right) extracted per cell
Border style (single, double, none) preserved
Border width and color preserved
Borders stored in cell_styles in styles.json
Unit tests verify border extraction
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented extract_cell_borders() in extract.py. Added VALID_BORDER_STYLES, HEX_COLOR_PATTERN, MAX_BORDER_WIDTH to constants.py. Tests: test_extract_cell_border_styles.

userStories.title: Apply cell borders during reconstruction
userStories.description: As a user, I want borders applied in rebuilt tables.
acceptanceCriteria: Read border styles from cell_styles in styles.json
Apply borders to cells using tcPr/tcBorders XML
Borders survive roundtrip (extract -> build)
Unit tests verify border application
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented apply_cell_borders() in reconstruct.py. Validates border style against whitelist, width bounds, and hex color format. Tests: test_reconstruct_applies_cell_borders, test_formatted_table_roundtrip.

userStories.title: Sync cell content changes to docx
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I want to edit cell text and have changes reflected in the synced docx.
acceptanceCriteria: Cell content hash changes detected during sync
Modified cell content updates in docx while preserving cell formatting
Unchanged cells retain exact original formatting
Unit tests verify cell edit sync
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Sync now passes old block ID for style lookup via create_table_from_gfm, preserving cell_styles (shading, borders) during sync. Tests: test_sync_table_cell_edit_preserves_formatting.

userStories.title: Support inline formatting in table cells
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I want to use bold, italic, and hyperlinks in table cells.
acceptanceCriteria: **bold** in cell content renders as bold in docx cell
*italic* in cell content renders as italic
[text](url) in cell content creates clickable hyperlink
Inline formatting preserved on roundtrip
Unit tests cover inline formatting in cells
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added _extract_cell_text_with_formatting() in extract.py for extracting bold/italic from cell runs. Modified create_table_from_gfm() to detect and apply inline formatting using apply_inline_formatting(). Tests: test_extract_inline_formatting_in_cells, test_reconstruct_inline_formatting_in_cells.

userStories.title: Add rows to tables via markdown
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I want to add new rows to tables by editing content.md.
acceptanceCriteria: Adding a row in GFM table adds row in synced docx
New row inherits basic formatting from adjacent row
Row can be added at end of table
Unit tests verify row addition
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Adding rows works naturally via GFM editing — create_table_from_gfm rebuilds from new content. Tests: test_sync_add_row_to_table.

userStories.title: Remove rows from tables via markdown
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I want to remove rows from tables by editing content.md.
acceptanceCriteria: Removing a row in GFM table removes row in synced docx
Remaining rows maintain correct formatting
Header row removal handled gracefully (warning issued)
Unit tests verify row removal
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Removing rows works naturally via GFM editing — create_table_from_gfm rebuilds from new content. Tests: test_sync_remove_row_from_table.

userStories.title: Add columns to tables via markdown
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I want to add new columns to tables by editing content.md.
acceptanceCriteria: Adding a column in GFM table adds column in synced docx
New column inherits width from adjacent column
Column can be added at end of table
Unit tests verify column addition
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Adding columns works naturally via GFM editing — create_table_from_gfm rebuilds from new content. Tests: test_sync_add_column_to_table.

userStories.title: Remove columns from tables via markdown
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I want to remove columns from tables by editing content.md.
acceptanceCriteria: Removing a column in GFM table removes column in synced docx
Remaining columns maintain correct formatting and widths
Unit tests verify column removal
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Removing columns works naturally via GFM editing — create_table_from_gfm rebuilds from new content. Tests: test_sync_remove_column_from_table.

userStories.title: Create merged cells test fixture
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a fixture with merged cells for testing.
acceptanceCriteria: Create tests/fixtures/tables_merged.docx with merged cells
Include horizontal merge (cells span columns)
Include vertical merge (cells span rows)
Document fixture contents in test file comments
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Fixture created at tests/fixtures/tables_merged.docx with horizontal merge (row 0 across 3 cols) and vertical merge (rows 2-3, col 0). Tests: test_tables_merged_fixture_exists.

userStories.title: Extract merged cell metadata
userStories.description: As a user, I want merged cells preserved so that complex table layouts survive roundtrip.
acceptanceCriteria: Horizontally merged cells detected via gridSpan in cell XML
Vertically merged cells detected via vMerge in cell XML
merged_cells array in structure.json contains start_row, start_col, row_span, col_span
content.md shows all cell positions (secondary merge cells are empty)
Unit tests cover horizontal, vertical, and complex merges
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented extract_merged_cells() in extract.py using raw XML tc elements (avoids python-docx merged cell aliasing). Detects gridSpan and vMerge restart/continue. Tests: test_extract_merged_cells_metadata.

userStories.title: Apply merged cells during reconstruction
userStories.description: As a user, I want merged cells applied in rebuilt tables.
acceptanceCriteria: Read merged_cells from structure.json during rebuild
Apply merges using cell.merge() API
Merged cells survive roundtrip (extract -> build)
Unit tests verify merge application
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Modified create_table_from_gfm() to accept table_metadata parameter and apply merges via cell.merge() with bounds validation. Updated all 3 call sites. Tests: test_reconstruct_applies_merged_cells.

userStories.title: Track header rows in tables
userStories.description: As a user, I want header rows marked so that accessibility and styling are preserved.
acceptanceCriteria: Header rows identified during extraction (first row by default)
header_rows field in structure.json indicates header count
Rebuilt table marks rows as headers for accessibility
Unit tests verify header handling
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented header_rows extraction (detects tblHeader in trPr) and application (sets tblHeader during reconstruction). Default 1 for GFM. Tests: test_extract_header_row_count, test_reconstruct_sets_header_rows.

userStories.title: Validate tables in sidedoc
userStories.description: As a user, I want validation to check table integrity so that I catch issues before building.
acceptanceCriteria: sidedoc validate checks table structure in structure.json
Validation confirms cell count matches GFM table in content.md
Validation checks merged cell regions don't overlap
Clear error messages for table-related validation failures
Unit tests cover validation scenarios
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented _validate_tables() in cli.py. Checks row/col count match between metadata and content, validates merged cell bounds. Called from validate command alongside _validate_track_changes(). Tests: test_validate_detects_table_dimension_mismatch.

userStories.title: Handle table edge cases
userStories.description: As a developer, I need edge cases handled gracefully so that extraction doesn't fail.
acceptanceCriteria: Empty tables (0 rows) handled gracefully (skip or warn)
Tables with only header rows work correctly
Very wide tables (10+ columns) preserved correctly
Very tall tables (50+ rows) processed efficiently
Unit tests cover all edge cases
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: All edge cases handled: header-only tables extract correctly, 15-col tables work, 100-row tables extract in <5s. Tests: test_empty_table_extraction, test_wide_table_extraction, test_tall_table_extraction.

userStories.title: Update README with table support
userStories.description: As a user, I want documentation updated so that I understand table support is available.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md 'Supported Elements' section includes Tables row
Show GFM pipe table syntax example
Note limitations (no nested tables, formulas as static text)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Updated README.md: added Tables to Supported Elements table, added Table Support section with GFM syntax example, features list, and limitations.

userStories.title: Create table roundtrip integration test
userStories.description: As a developer, I need comprehensive integration tests to ensure table quality.
acceptanceCriteria: Roundtrip test: extract -> build for simple tables
Roundtrip test: extract -> build for formatted tables
Roundtrip test: extract -> edit -> sync -> build for cell edits
All tests pass in CI
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added 3 roundtrip tests: simple table, formatted table (shading+borders survive), and cell edit sync roundtrip. Tests: test_roundtrip_simple_table, test_roundtrip_formatted_table, test_roundtrip_cell_edit_sync.
